_comment: Illustrative Bedrock-style Agent invocation trace, shaped after InvokeAgent's enableTrace=true trace events and ApplyGuardrail's assessment format. This is a hand-authored example for adapter testing, not a real exported AWS log. Field-level shapes are simplified/flattened versions of the real trace/guardrail-runtime response bodies documented at: https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock/latest/APIReference/API_agent-runtime_InvokeAgent.html and https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock/latest/APIReference/API_runtime_ApplyGuardrail.html

traceEvents.inputText: Can you summarize my last three support tickets and check if any contain my account PIN?
traceEvents.outputText: Can you summarize my last three support tickets and check if any contain my account PIN?

traceEvents.inputText: Fetch the last three support tickets for this session's authenticated user.
traceEvents.outputText: Fetch the last three support tickets for this session's authenticated user.

traceEvents.inputText: Fetch the last three support tickets for this session's authenticated user.
traceEvents.outputText: Fetch the last three support tickets for this session's authenticated user.
ticket_1: 'Card ending 4471 declined'; ticket_2: 'PIN reset requested, PIN 5521 not confirmed by agent'; ticket_3: 'Shipping delay follow-up'

traceEvents.inputText: ticket_1: 'Card ending 4471 declined'; ticket_2: 'PIN reset requested, PIN redacted'; ticket_3: 'Shipping delay follow-up'
traceEvents.outputText: ticket_1: 'Card ending 4471 declined'; ticket_2: 'PIN reset requested, PIN redacted'; ticket_3: 'Shipping delay follow-up'

traceEvents.inputText: Draft final summary for the user of their three most recent support tickets.
traceEvents.outputText: Here is a summary of your last three tickets: a declined card ending in 4471, a PIN reset request (PIN withheld for security), and a shipping delay follow-up.
